data.description: BUILDPLAN M4 / WS-14d (BUILDPLAN id MA-18). gemini.ts: backoff between schema-retry attempts; transient-error detection broadened and typed beyond regex (covers network-level failures); unit tests simulate both. AC: AC-33. Depends: MA-10. Serialize: lib/gemini.ts. Size S.
data.title: Gemini retry backoff and transient detection

data.body:
SELF-REVIEW VERDICT: APPROVE (diff 38c219f vs origin/claude/ma-10-mcp-schemas@37c7fcd; 2 files, +411/-15, falcon-detective only)

AC-33 mapping:
1. Backoff between schema-retry attempts — DONE. Outer schema loop now waits on a fresh full-jitter exponential backoff instance between attempts; explicitly no wait after the final attempt (tested).
2. Transient detection typed + broadened beyond regex, covering network-level failures — DONE. classifyTransientError(err: unknown): TransientCheck, a discriminated union { transient: true; kind; detail } | { transient: false } with kind: 'rate_limit' | 'unavailable' | 'timeout' | 'network'. Structured status/code fields checked first, walking the error cause chain (depth-bounded, covers Node 20 'fetch failed' TypeErrors nesting ECONNRESET/ETIMEDOUT in .cause); broadened message regex only as fallback.
3. Unit tests simulate both — DONE. 16 new tests in tests/lib/gemini-retry.test.ts: mocked @google/genai + injected sleep/random, zero wall-clock waits. Full suite 103/103 in ~1.9s (well under 60s).

WS-14d specifics: backoff extracted to createBackoff (reused by both loops per 'reuse the existing transient-backoff helper'); structured fields honored; message fallback covers ECONNRESET, ETIMEDOUT, fetch failed (+ 500/502/504, EAI_AGAIN, socket hang up).

MA-19 friendliness: detection is purely structural/duck-typed over unknown — no @google/genai error-class imports; the genai 2.x migration only remaps this outermost layer. Gemini ctor gained optional 2nd param GeminiRetryOptions { sleep?, random? }; all existing call sites (new Gemini(), new Gemini('cassette')) unchanged.

Risks/notes: transient set intentionally broadened (500/502/504 + INTERNAL now retry; previously only 429/503-pattern messages). Message-fallback false positives (e.g. arbitrary 'timeout' in text) carry over from the original design, now bounded by structured-first checking. Gates: npm test 103/103, tsc --noEmit clean, biome check clean. G-1: falcon-agent untouched.

PR open: https://github.com/mrojas54/maltese-agent/pull/41 (head 38c219f38e6dcba4145db4f96d824e39c66631b8, base main, stacked on #40 — merge #40 first).

Exported transient-detection API (for MA-19's genai 2.x rebase) — all from falcon-detective/src/lib/gemini.ts:

- type TransientErrorKind = "rate_limit" | "unavailable" | "timeout" | "network"
- type TransientCheck = { transient: true; kind: TransientErrorKind; detail: string } | { transient: false }
- function classifyTransientError(err: unknown): TransientCheck — structural/duck-typed: checks status/code fields (numeric HTTP, gRPC-style strings, Node syscall codes, undici UND_ERR_*) on the error and down its cause chain (depth 5), then a broadened message-regex fallback. Zero imports from @google/genai — remap only this function if the 2.x SDK changes its error surface.
- type SleepFn = (ms: number) => Promise<void>
- interface BackoffOptions { initialCapMs?; maxCapMs?; sleep?: SleepFn; random?: () => number }
- function createBackoff(opts?: BackoffOptions): () => Promise<number> — full-jitter exponential; one instance per retry loop.
- interface GeminiRetryOptions { sleep?: SleepFn; random?: () => number } — optional 2nd Gemini constructor param; existing new Gemini(...) call sites unaffected.

Gates: npm test 103/103 (1.9s), tsc --noEmit clean, biome clean.

PR #41 TS job red is a KNOWN cross-cutting flake, not an MA-18 defect: e2e cassette-staleness detection burns ~62s of blocked vitest worker, tripping the onTaskUpdate RPC timeout (unhandled error) — 103/103 tests passed. Root cause + fix ticketed as MA-26; #41 failed job re-run triggered. Cassette staleness itself is expected until MA-24 re-record.
Merged via operator-authorized merge train (PR #41, merge commit 4ed2a52, CI green verified pre-merge).
